company.name: Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd
company.description: Motilal Oswal Financial Services, founded in 1987 by Motilal Oswal and Raamdeo Agrawal, has evolved from a research-driven equity broking house into one of India's most comprehensive financial services platforms. The company spans Capital Markets, Asset & Wealth Management, Housing Finance, and Alternative Investments, serving over 1,600,000 customers across 550+ cities through 2,500+ business locations. Research coverage of 300+ companies remains the firm's enduring competitive moat.

blocks.content: BUY with SAARTHI Score of 72/100. Motilal Oswal Financial Services represents a compelling investment opportunity driven by its accelerating transformation from a cyclical broking firm into a high-quality, annuity-driven financial services platform. Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) now constitutes the relevant figure of total net revenues — with management guiding for further increases — fundamentally re-rating the quality of the earnings base. blocks.content: Indian Financial Services is a strategic shock absorber for the growth story. India is expected to have the relevant figure HNWIs by 2027 and become the fourth-largest private wealth market by 2028, with a the relevant figure projected rise in UHNWIs. The financial services sector is projected to nearly double profits by FY30, led by NBFCs growing at the relevant figure annually. Mutual fund penetration at just 5–the relevant figure reflects substantial latent growth opportunity. Private credit hit a record the relevant figure billion in H1 2025, up the relevant figure YoY.

blocks.content: Motilal Oswal Financial Services, founded in 1987 by Motilal Oswal and Raamdeo Agrawal, has evolved from a research-driven equity broking house into one of India's most comprehensive financial services platforms. The company spans Capital Markets, Asset & Wealth Management, Housing Finance, and Alternative Investments, serving over 1,600,000 customers across 550+ cities through 2,500+ business locations. Research coverage of 300+ companies remains the firm's enduring competitive moat.
Six themes underpinning the investment view.

blocks.content: Founder-led leadership with deep institutional DNA.

Recurring regulatory matters are operational rather than fraudulent. Despite SEBI interventions, structural lapses suggest deeper governance attention needed in risk management. Pattern warrants monitoring but does not impair the business model.
Recent quarterly trajectory showing the variability inherent to the cyclical revenue base.
Margin and return profile across the forecast period.

Competitive intensity from discount brokers and fintech platforms compressing yields
Forensic / governance: recurring SEBI matters require monitoring (Monitor Closely category)
Mark-to-market volatility on the proprietary book
Housing finance asset-quality slippage in stressed credit cycles
